gauge.notes: The route uses USGS 04086600 Milwaukee River near Cedarburg, WI, a direct same-river gauge downstream of the Newburg-to-Waubedonia corridor. Wisconsin River Trips and Miles Paddled both use the Cedarburg gauge for this route family.

thresholdSupport.notes: Wisconsin River Trips publishes route-specific Cedarburg-gauge guidance for the Newburg segment: 200-400 cfs for best clarity, 400-600 cfs still likely fine, and 600+ cfs increasingly less attractive though probably doable. Miles Paddled documents the full Newburg-to-Waubedonia route at about 390 cfs and treats that as just past too low, while Wisconsin River Trips' adjacent Waubeka-to-Waubedonia page supports a conservative 801+ cfs high-water cutoff.

endpoints.putIn: Fireman's Park / Main Street bridge, Newburg
endpoints.coordinateSource: Existing V2 coordinates align with the named Fireman's Park / Main Street bridge put-in described by Wisconsin River Trips, Miles Paddled, and Riveredge Nature Center, plus Ozaukee County's Waubedonia Park canoe/kayak launch map and park page.

candidates.notes: Completed 2026-05-25 as a route-quality reconciliation rather than a duplicate route add. The app route already existed with endpoint coordinates and a Commons gallery image, but this pass upgraded the profile from minimum-only to a two-sided Cedarburg-gauge model and added the missing structured ledger record. The app uses 390-600 cfs as the preferred full-route window, 200 cfs as the hard low mark, and 801+ cfs as the broad-audience high cutoff.

gauge.notes: The route uses USGS 04025500 Bois Brule River at Brule, WI. Wisconsin Trail Guide says the referenced gauge is located in this segment and gives an accurate indication of current conditions; American Whitewater also ties the reach to the Brule gauge.

thresholdSupport.notes: Wisconsin Trail Guide publishes route-specific BB1 bands: below 125 cfs not recommended, 125-200 cfs low runnable, 200-350 cfs medium runnable for novice paddlers, 350-600 cfs high runnable, and 600-1000 cfs not recommended for novice paddlers. American Whitewater corroborates 125 cfs as the low boatable floor for the same gauge family.

endpoints.coordinateSource: Existing V2 coordinates align with the named Stone's Bridge and Winneboujou landings described by Wisconsin DNR and Wisconsin Trail Guide, which documents both as designated Brule River canoe landings with parking and facilities.

candidates.notes: Reconciled 2026-05-25 after finding the route already present in app data but absent from the structured ledger and image audit. This route clears the Wisconsin quality bar with DNR-designated public landings, a direct same-segment USGS gauge, route-specific Wisconsin Trail Guide flow bands, Wisconsin River Trips route confirmation, and AW gauge corroboration. A reusable Commons Brule River photo near Winneboujou and a public-domain USGS gauge photo were found, but sandbox network blocked direct asset download, so no gallery entry was wired.
